resources.string: Watches the LAN neighbor table for the classic signs of a man-in-the-middle: your router’s MAC address changing, one device claiming several IPs, or a bogus gateway address. It detects and warns — it cannot block or prevent ARP spoofing.
Reading the neighbor table needs Shizuku

On Android 10 and newer, a normal app can’t read the kernel ARP/neighbor table. Grant the optional Shizuku access and this screen can scan for spoofing. It stays detect-only — nothing is blocked, and no VPN slot is used.
Reads the neighbor table once and checks it against your saved gateway baseline.
No changed gateway MAC, duplicate MACs, or bogus gateway address in the current table.
The table came back empty. Try again after some network activity.
No baseline recorded. On a network you trust, set a baseline so a later gateway-MAC change is flagged.

Background scans are best-effort: if Shizuku isn’t bound in the background, the check records “could not scan” rather than a false all-clear. Android may run these a little early or late to save power.
The watch still runs and records each check below, but it can’t alert you until you allow notifications.

This is a read-and-warn tool. It reads only the on-device neighbor table through the optional Shizuku shell, keeps a gateway MAC baseline on this device only, and can’t stop an attacker — use it to notice and react.
